chemistry.scope_summary: Wide range of phenolic substrates converted to benzoquinones and p-quinols. Electron-rich phenols (e.g., methoxyphenols) gave excellent yields. Unsubstituted phenol and electron-deficient phenols remained unreacted. Method also works for naphthols and produces vitamin precursors like trimethylbenzoquinone (vitamin E precursor) and menadione (vitamin K3).

optimization_table.key_findings: 4:1 MeOH:H2O solvent with air/10 bar gave 96% yield

light_source.position_relative_to_reactor: around LED lamp allowing to optically insert reactor

figures.caption: Scheme 1  Aerobic flow oxygenation of phenols using pressurized air, visible light  and an organic photocatalyst: a) Synthesis of 1,4-benzoquinones and related  quinoidal products. b) Simplified layout of the device to control the principal pro- cess parameters (numbered in blue) in a continuous-flow photoreactor with visi- ble light and pressurized O 2  (air).   : micromixer; λ: irradiation wavel | Scheme 2 .   DFT computations performed with M06-2X/6-311+G(3df,2p)//M06/6- 311+G(d,p); PCM for water. a)   [4+2]-Cycloaddition of 2,3,5- or 2,3,6-TMP with  singlet oxygen and subsequent TMBQ formation. b) Transition states for 2,3,6- TMP + O 2 ( 1 Δ g ) →  M1  (in green) and 2,3,5-TMP + O 2 ( 1 Δ g ) →  M1  (in orange); Gibbs  free energy profile for 2,3,6-TMP vs. 2,3,5-TMP in kJmol −1   at 298 K

DFT computations performed with M06-2X/6-311+G(3df,2p)//M06/6- 311+G(d,p); PCM for water. a)   [4+2]-Cycloaddition of 2,3,5- or 2,3,6-TMP with  singlet oxygen and subsequent TMBQ formation. b) Transition states for 2,3,6- TMP + O 2 ( 1 Δ g ) →  M1  (in green) and 2,3,5-TMP + O 2 ( 1 Δ g ) →  M1  (in orange); Gibbs  free energy profile for 2,3,6-TMP vs. 2,3,5-TMP in kJmol −1   at 298 K

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Low yield (23%) in batch due to limited oxygen solubility and light penetration
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Continuous flow enables pressurized air, better mass transfer, and uniform light exposure
translation_logic.what_changed: Solvent (CD3CN to MeOH:H2O 4:1), atmosphere (O2-saturated to pressurized air at 10 bar), residence time (30 to 16 min)
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Photocatalyst (methylene blue), reaction mechanism (singlet oxygen generation)
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Use of pressurized air instead of pure oxygen reduces explosion hazard

figure_descriptions: Scheme 1 shows both reaction and simplified flow reactor layout with micromixer and LED irradiation
Scheme 2 shows DFT calculations and mechanism with singlet oxygen cycloaddition

notes: High confidence extraction with clear batch-to-flow comparison. Key improvement: 23% to 96% yield with shorter reaction time. Flow enables pressurized air use and better mass transfer.
